description: anchoring_existence passes (the commitment is externally anchored and bound to the admission) but anchoring_precedence fails: the anchor carries no forward, pre-outcome stamp (precedence=false), so it proves the commitment EXISTS, not that it was made BEFORE the outcome. The anchor's block_time looking earlier than the outcome is not enough — a backfilled stamp can sit on an earlier block yet have been minted after the fact. Existence and precedence are different questions; this fixture isolates the precedence break.

trust_policy.policy: An admission verdict counts as independent iff its signing pubkey is in independent_verifier_pubkeys AND is not the actor/executor pubkey in the chain.

signer_identity_evidence.publication_pointer: https://api.babyblueviper.com/ledger (verifier_pubkey is self-described in the proof)

accepted_anchor_point.note: Confirmed anchor: the commitment provably EXISTS. But precedence=false — this is a post-hoc / backfilled stamp with no forward pre-outcome commitment, so it does not establish the commitment was made BEFORE the terminal outcome. block_time appearing earlier than the outcome is not sufficient on its own.
